title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The dishwasher heating element or its circuit has failed, preventing water from reaching the correct temperature.
fullDescription: Error E01 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the control module has detected that water temperature is not rising as expected. As a BSH group brand alongside Bosch and Thermador, Gaggenau shares the same diagnostic code system. The heating element, NTC temperature sensor, relay on the control board, or associated wiring may be at fault.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out or has an open circuit
NTC temperature sensor is providing incorrect readings
Control board heater relay has failed
Wiring harness between heater and control board is damaged
Thermal fuse on the heating circuit has tripped

symptoms: Dishes come out cold and not properly cleaned
Water does not feel warm during wash phase
E01 displayed on the control panel
Cycle takes significantly longer than normal

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher and disconnect power at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and run a test cycle.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heating element at the bottom of the tub. Measure resistance across the terminals with a multimeter. A good element reads 10-30 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor near the sump. Disconnect and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 50k ohms.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and connections
fixSteps.instruction: Examine wiring between the heater, sensor, and control board. Look for burned, corroded, or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the element tested faulty, remove the lower spray arm, disconnect wiring, remove mounting hardware, and install the new element.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is suspected. Gaggenau repairs typically require authorized BSH service for warranty coverage.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E01 error heating element fix

faq.q: Is the Gaggenau E01 the same as the Bosch E01?
faq.a: Yes. Gaggenau, Bosch, and Thermador are all BSH group brands sharing the same diagnostic error system.

faq.q: Can I still use the dishwasher with E01?
faq.a: It may run with cold water, but cleaning and sanitization will be very poor. Repair promptly.

faq.q: Why is Gaggenau repair more expensive?
faq.a: Gaggenau is a premium BSH brand. While parts are often shared with Bosch, authorized service rates may be higher.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor is sending out-of-range readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error E02 on Gaggenau dishwashers signals a malfunction in the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) sensor. The control board is receiving temperature readings outside the expected range, preventing proper water temperature regulation. This BSH group code is identical across Gaggenau, Bosch, and Thermador dishwashers.

causes.cause: NTC temperature sensor has failed or drifted
Wiring to the NTC sensor is broken or corroded
Connector on the control board is loose
Control board input circuit is damaged

symptoms: E02 error code on the display

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 5 minutes. Reconnect and start a cycle. If E02 returns, proceed to diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Locate the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The sensor is in the sump area. Remove the lower rack and spray arm to access the sump cover.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read around 50k ohms. Test in warm water to verify it decreases smoothly.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If faulty, remove the old sensor from the sump housing, insert the new one, and reconnect the harness.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not fix E02, the control board needs professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E02 NTC sensor replacement

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: NTC stands for Negative Temperature Coefficient. The sensor's resistance decreases as temperature increases, allowing the control board to monitor water temperature.

faq.q: Can I use a Bosch NTC sensor in my Gaggenau?
faq.a: Yes. Gaggenau and Bosch share the same BSH parts. The NTC sensor part number is identical.

faq.q: Will E02 cause water damage?
faq.a: No. E02 stops the cycle to prevent improper operation but does not cause leaks or flooding.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not fill with enough water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E03 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the tub did not reach the required water level within the fill timeout period. The water supply, inlet valve, inlet filter, or water level sensor may be responsible.

causes.cause: Water supply valve is closed or restricted
Water inlet valve is clogged or defective
Inlet hose is kinked or blocked
Water level sensor or pressure switch is faulty

symptoms: Dishwasher does not start the wash cycle
Only a small amount of water in the tub
Cycle starts but stops after a few minutes

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the supply valve under the sink is fully open. Run the kitchen faucet to confirm pressure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the supply hose for kinks or blockages. Straighten or replace if needed.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the supply hose and clean the mesh filter at the inlet valve.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the inlet valve solenoid resistance. It should read 500-1500 ohms. No continuity means replacement is needed.

whenToCallPro: If supply is normal and the valve tests good, the water level sensor or control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E03 water fill error fix

faq.q: What is the minimum water pressure for a Gaggenau dishwasher?
faq.a: Gaggenau dishwashers require a minimum of 20 PSI (1.4 bar) water supply pressure for proper filling.

faq.q: Can a clogged filter cause E03?
faq.a: Yes. The small mesh filter at the inlet valve can become clogged with mineral deposits, restricting water flow.

faq.q: Is E03 common?
faq.a: E03 is one of the most frequent error codes and usually has a simple fix related to water supply issues.

title: Heating Relay Circuit Failure
shortDescription: The control board's heating relay has failed, preventing the heater from activating.
fullDescription: Error E09 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the heating relay on the main control board has failed. This is a board-level fault where the relay cannot switch power to the heating element. The board needs repair or replacement.

causes.cause: Heating relay on the control board has failed
Relay solder joint has cracked from thermal cycling
Power surge damaged the relay
Shorted heating element burned out the relay

symptoms: Water never heats during any cycle
E01 may have appeared before E09

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 10 minutes. If E09 returns immediately, the relay has permanently failed.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element for shorts
fixSteps.instruction: Before replacing the board, test the element for shorts to ground. A shorted element will damage a new board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board relay
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the door panel to access the board. Look for burn marks or discoloration around the relay area.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct replacement board. Photograph all connections, disconnect, and install the new board.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement is recommended for professional service, especially on premium Gaggenau appliances.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E09 control board relay fix

faq.q: Can the relay be repaired instead of replacing the board?
faq.a: Some specialized appliance repair shops can resolder relays. This is cheaper but not all technicians offer this service.

faq.q: Is the Gaggenau control board the same as Bosch?
faq.a: Many Gaggenau and Bosch dishwashers use the same control board. Verify the part number for your specific model.

faq.q: How do I protect against future relay failure?
faq.a: Install a surge protector on the dishwasher circuit. Power surges are a leading cause of relay damage.

title: AquaStop Water in Base Pan
shortDescription: The AquaStop flood protection system has detected water in the base pan beneath the tub.
fullDescription: Error E15 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the float switch in the base pan has risen due to water accumulation, triggering the AquaStop flood protection. The drain pump activates to remove water and the dishwasher is locked out until the water is cleared and the leak fixed.

causes.cause: Leaking tub gasket or spray arm seal
Loose or cracked hose connection inside the machine
Sump seal has deteriorated
AquaStop float switch is stuck
Condensation buildup from short cool cycles

symptoms: Drain pump runs continuously even when off
Dishwasher will not start any cycle
Musty smell from the base area

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power and water
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker and close the water supply valve before inspection.

fixSteps.title: Tilt to drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the dishwasher forward about 45 degrees to drain water from the base pan into towels. This resets the float switch.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the base panel and dry remaining water. With the base dry, reconnect power and water, run a short cycle, and watch with a flashlight to identify the leak.

fixSteps.title: Repair the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Based on the identified source, tighten hose clamps, replace gaskets, or repair seals as needed.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot identify the leak source, or if the sump housing is cracked, professional service is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E15 AquaStop leak fix

faq.q: How do I reset E15 on my Gaggenau dishwasher?
faq.a: Tilt the machine forward about 45 degrees to drain the base pan. The float switch resets when water is removed. You must also fix the leak.

faq.q: What is AquaStop?
faq.a: AquaStop is BSH's flood protection system using a float switch and automatic shutoff valve to prevent water damage from dishwasher leaks.

faq.q: Can E15 trigger from condensation?
faq.a: Rarely. If the dishwasher runs many short cool cycles, condensation can slowly accumulate. Running a full hot cycle periodically helps.

title: Clogged Filter or Drain Issue
shortDescription: The dishwasher cannot drain properly due to a clogged filter or drain system blockage.
fullDescription: Error E22 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the tub is not draining within the expected time. The filter assembly, drain pump, drain hose, or garbage disposal connection may be blocked. This is one of the most common Gaggenau dishwasher errors.

causes.cause: Filter assembly is clogged with food debris
Drain pump impeller is jammed
Drain hose is kinked or clogged
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the tub

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack and filter assembly. Clean all filter components under running water with a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks or blockages. Disconnect and flush if needed.

fixSteps.title: Check garbage disposal connection
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to a disposal, ensure the knockout plug was removed. Run the disposal to clear any blockage.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter cover to access the pump impeller. Remove any glass or debris jamming it.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve E22 and the drain pump needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E22 drain clog fix

faq.q: How often should I clean my Gaggenau dishwasher filter?
faq.a: Clean the filter at least monthly. Weekly if you wash heavily soiled dishes without pre-rinsing.

faq.q: Can E22 cause flooding?
faq.a: E22 itself prevents flooding by stopping the cycle. However, standing water can cause odors and mold if not addressed.

faq.q: My dishwasher drains slowly but no error appears. Should I worry?
faq.a: Yes. Slow draining often precedes E22. Clean the filter and check the drain hose proactively.

title: Drain Hose Blockage
shortDescription: The drain hose path is blocked or improperly installed, preventing water evacuation.
fullDescription: Error E24 on Gaggenau dishwashers specifically indicates a drain hose routing or connection problem. The hose may be kinked, the connection to the drain or disposal may be blocked, or the installation lacks a proper high loop or air gap.

causes.cause: Drain hose is kinked behind the dishwasher
Drain connection point is blocked
No high loop in the drain hose installation
Air gap device is clogged

symptoms: Water backs up into the tub
Water leaks from under the sink
Cycle completes but water remains in tub

fixSteps.title: Inspect drain hose routing
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher forward to inspect the drain hose. Straighten any kinks or sharp bends.

fixSteps.title: Ensure proper high loop
fixSteps.instruction: The drain hose must loop up to the underside of the countertop before descending to the drain. Secure with a clip.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain connection
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the hose from the disposal or drain pipe. Clear buildup from both the hose and connection point.

fixSteps.title: Clean air gap if present
fixSteps.instruction: If your installation has an air gap on the countertop, remove the cap and clean debris inside.

whenToCallPro: If the drain path is clear but E24 persists, the drain pump may be weakening.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E24 drain hose blockage fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E22 and E24?
faq.a: E22 is a general drain fault often from a clogged filter or pump. E24 specifically indicates a drain hose routing or connection issue.

faq.q: Why does my Gaggenau dishwasher need a high loop?
faq.a: The high loop prevents dirty water from siphoning back into the dishwasher from the drain line. It is required by most plumbing codes.

faq.q: Can the drain hose be too long?
faq.a: Yes. The drain hose should not exceed 10 feet total. Longer runs reduce drain pump effectiveness.

title: Drain Pump Impeller Blocked
shortDescription: The drain pump impeller is obstructed by debris, preventing water evacuation.
fullDescription: Error E25 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the drain pump impeller is physically blocked. Common obstructions include broken glass, food particles, seeds, or small utensils that have bypassed the filter system. The pump cannot rotate and water remains in the tub.

causes.cause: Broken glass lodged in the pump impeller
Food debris accumulated around the impeller
Pump cover not properly seated
Drain pump motor has seized

symptoms: No pump motor sound during drain
Intermittent draining that worsens over time

fixSteps.title: Access the pump area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack and filter assembly. Remove the sump cover to expose the drain pump impeller.

fixSteps.title: Remove debris
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully remove glass, food, or foreign objects from around the impeller. Wear gloves for protection.

fixSteps.title: Verify free rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Gently spin the impeller by hand. It should rotate freely. If stiff, the motor bearings may have failed.

fixSteps.title: Reassemble and test
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the sump cover, filter, and rack. Run a drain cycle to confirm E25 is resolved.

whenToCallPro: If the impeller does not spin freely after clearing debris, the pump motor has likely failed.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E25 drain pump blocked fix

faq.q: How do small items reach the drain pump?
faq.a: Items can pass through gaps in the filter system. Always scrape dishes before loading and keep the filter clean.

faq.q: Can E25 damage the drain pump?
faq.a: Running the pump repeatedly against a blockage can burn out the motor over time. Clear the blockage promptly.

faq.q: Is E25 preventable?
faq.a: Yes. Regular filter cleaning and proper dish scraping before loading prevent most E25 occurrences.

title: Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water flow sensor detects insufficient circulation through the spray system.
fullDescription: Error E04 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the flow sensor is not detecting adequate water circulation. The spray arms may be clogged, the circulation pump may be impaired, or the flow sensor itself may be faulty.

causes.cause: Spray arms are clogged with deposits
Circulation pump impeller is damaged
Flow sensor is faulty
Sump filter is severely clogged

symptoms: Cycle completes but dishes are dry (no water reached them)

fixSteps.title: Clean spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove both spray arms. Clear all nozzle holes with a toothpick. Rinse under running water.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean all filter sections thoroughly under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: With filters removed, inspect the pump area for debris. Remove any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Test the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the flow sensor on the pump housing. Check wiring and test resistance with a multimeter.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve E04 and the pump or sensor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E04 flow sensor spray arm fix

faq.q: Can hard water cause E04?
faq.a: Yes. Mineral deposits from hard water clog spray arm nozzles and restrict flow. Use a dishwasher cleaner monthly.

faq.q: How often should spray arms be cleaned?
faq.a: Clean spray arms every 2-3 months, or monthly in hard water areas.

faq.q: Is E04 always serious?
faq.a: Often it is a simple fix like cleaning. Only if the pump or sensor has failed does it require parts replacement.

title: Overfill Protection Activated
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected excess water in the tub beyond the maximum level.
fullDescription: Error E17 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the water level has exceeded the maximum threshold. The drain pump activates to remove excess water. A stuck inlet valve, faulty pressure switch, or blocked drain causing residual water buildup may be responsible.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve is stuck open
Pressure switch or water level sensor is faulty
Drain hose partially blocked causing water buildup
Flow meter not counting accurately

symptoms: Excess water visible when door is opened
Drain pump runs to remove water
Water on the floor around the dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Shut off water immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Close the water supply valve under the sink to stop water entering. Then disconnect power.

fixSteps.title: Drain excess water
fixSteps.instruction: Reconnect power briefly to let the drain pump run, or bail water from the tub manually.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With supply off and power disconnected, check if water still drips through the inlet valve. If so, the solenoid is stuck open.

fixSteps.title: Check drain path
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for clogs or kinks. Ensure proper high loop installation.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is stuck open, replace it promptly to prevent flooding.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E17 overfill inlet valve fix

faq.q: Is E17 an emergency?
faq.a: It can be. A stuck inlet valve means water continues entering. Shut off the supply valve immediately.

faq.q: Can a blocked drain cause E17?
faq.a: Yes. If water cannot drain properly between cycles, residual water accumulates and triggers the overfill sensor.

faq.q: How do I prevent E17?
faq.a: Maintain the drain path, clean filters regularly, and have the inlet valve inspected if it is more than 5 years old.

title: Overflow Protection Activated
shortDescription: The flood protection system detected excess water in the base pan beneath the tub.
fullDescription: Error E05 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the AquaStop base pan float switch has triggered due to water accumulation beneath the dishwasher tub. This error is closely related to E15 and may appear depending on the specific model and control board version. The diagnosis and repair are essentially the same.

causes.cause: Leaking door seal or gasket
Loose hose connection inside the machine
Cracked sump housing
Float switch stuck or faulty

symptoms: Musty odor from under the machine

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power and water
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker and close the water supply valve.

fixSteps.title: Drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the dishwasher forward about 45 degrees to drain accumulated water. Place towels to catch water.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the base panel. Dry the base pan. Reconnect power and water, run a cycle, and watch for the leak with a flashlight.

fixSteps.title: Fix the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten loose hose clamps, replace damaged seals or gaskets, or repair the identified leak source.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot locate the leak or if the sump housing is cracked.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E05 flood protection leak fix

faq.q: Is E05 the same as E15?
faq.a: They are very similar. Both indicate water in the base pan. The specific code depends on the model and control board version.

faq.q: Will drying the base pan fix E05 permanently?
faq.a: Temporarily yes, but the underlying leak must be found and fixed or E05 will recur.

faq.q: Can I prevent base pan flooding?
faq.a: Regular inspection of door seals, hose connections, and prompt attention to any dripping can prevent base pan water accumulation.

title: Drying System Fault
shortDescription: The drying fan or drying system is not functioning, preventing proper dish drying.
fullDescription: Error E10 on Gaggenau dishwashers indicates the drying system has malfunctioned. Gaggenau uses various drying technologies including condensation drying with Zeolith technology on some models and fan-assisted drying on others. When the drying system fails, dishes remain wet after the cycle.

causes.cause: Drying fan motor has failed
Zeolith container is depleted or damaged (on Zeolith models)
Fan motor wiring has a loose connection
Control board drying circuit has failed

symptoms: Dishes are very wet after the drying phase
Excessive condensation on the inner door
Drying cycle seems to be skipped

fixSteps.title: Listen for the drying fan
fixSteps.instruction: Run a cycle and listen during the drying phase. If no fan sound is heard, the motor may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Access the drying system
fixSteps.instruction: The drying fan or Zeolith container is located in the door or side panel area. Remove the appropriate panel to access.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring and test for continuity with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drying component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty fan motor or drying system component. Reconnect wiring and reassemble.

whenToCallPro: Zeolith drying system issues should be handled by authorized BSH service. Fan motor replacement can be DIY.
videoSearchQuery: Gaggenau dishwasher E10 drying system fix

faq.q: What is Zeolith drying?
faq.a: Zeolith is a natural mineral that absorbs moisture and releases heat. Gaggenau uses it in some models for energy-efficient drying without a traditional heater.

faq.q: Can I still wash dishes with E10?
faq.a: Yes. Washing functions are not affected by E10. You will need to towel-dry dishes until the drying system is repaired.

faq.q: Does rinse aid help if E10 is active?
faq.a: Rinse aid helps water sheet off dishes but cannot substitute for the drying system. It will help somewhat but not solve the issue.
